reasoning: The failure is isolated to the connectivity and control plane between the O-DU and the near-RT RIC. The presence of packet loss and retransmissions on the E2 interface (uplink from O-DU to near-RT RIC) precedes and causes the observed latency increases and downstream effects on F1 and A1 interfaces. The non-RT RIC remains healthy, further isolating the failure to the O-DU, O-CU, and near-RT RIC components.
actionable_steps: 1. Inspect the physical and logical link between the O-DU and the near-RT RIC to identify sources of packet loss. 2. Verify the health of the E2 agent on the O-DU to ensure it is not dropping packets due to resource exhaustion. 3. Restart the E2AP (E2 Application Protocol) signaling processes on both the O-DU and the near-RT RIC to clear any stale sessions.
